name: Victoria Medina
special_instructions: Given her history of ear and respiratory infections, it's important to feed her in an upright position and ensure a clean environment - this includes disinfecting toys, sanitizing tables and maintaining overall hygiene. Down Syndrome. She may require special feeding accommodations, which will be communicated to the teacher as her feeding therapy progresses. If she's spending tummy time, make sure to place her on her own sheets.

name: Maxwell Marshall
special_instructions: Allergic to eggs and milk and almonds. Contact reactive also so will get localized hives if their allergen touches their skin. Allergic to all forms (ie: in baked items as well as the raw form). If known ingestion then Epi Pen and calling 911. Refer to the FARE allergy protocol: If large widespread hives, excessive coughing, vomiting, lethargy, OR breathing issues then EPI and 911 immediately. If 2 minor symptoms occur together then EPI + 911 (ie: localized hives + itchy mouth; nausea + sneezing, etc). Has past experience with swelling of lips with exposure. Anaphylactic.

name: Lennox Marshall
special_instructions: Allergic to eggs and milk. Contact reactive also so will get localized hives if their allergen touches their skin. Refer to the FARE allergy protocol: If large widespread hives, excessive coughing, vomiting, lethargy, OR breathing issues then EPI and 911 immediately. If 2 minor symptoms occur together then EPI + 911 (ie: localized hives + itchy mouth; nausea + sneezing, etc). Anaphylactic.

name: Koa White
special_instructions: History of febrile seizures as a toddler. Has not had one in over 2 years. Usually if he reaches 101/103 he will being to seize. Lay him on his side and begin a timer. Call 911. They will ask how long he has been seizing. Comfort with a soft voice until he comes to. If his eyes are rolling or he cannot answer he could still be seizing. Follow 911 instructions and call us parents

name: Smarika Botcha Nandwana
special_instructions: "She to ADPKS Type 1, is prone to UTI's, please inform mother if she is dehydrated or over-urinating, if Smarika appears swollen along her eyelids, the dorsal hands, feet and or genitals, is unwell with fever, painful and or frequent urination she should be seen urgently. In the interim, her kidneys remain healthy longer with adequate plain water intake, minimizing salt intake, and assuring she is not in stressful situations like outdoors in over 100’F for over 5 minutes at a time."

name: Phoenix Bates
special_instructions: Phoenix does not have anaphylaxis after gluten or dairy but she does have sensitivity to both and will have a delayed immune response resulting in rashes, stomach aches and other symptoms caused by the inflammatory response. She is currently in a protocol provided by her physical to repair the gut biome and then we will reintroduce both glute and dairy in about 3 months. For now, please abstain from both; notify mom if she consumes either.
